base_sentence: Internal AGN feedback processes drive powerful outflows that eject gas or heat circumgalactic gas, quenching star formation via starvation.
intro_sentence: Gas can be compressed along and at the peak of these massive outflows, leading to star formation activity known as positive AGN feedback ( Cresci2015TheMUSE ; Maiolino2017StarOutflow ; Gallagher2019WidespreadOutflows ; Shin2019Positive5728 ) .
qwen_reason: Intro asserts AGN feedback triggers star formation (positive), while base asserts it quenches star formation.

base_sentence: Gas removal and depletion, including disrupted accretion and stripping of circumgalactic reservoirs, suppress star formation by reducing cold gas supply.
intro_sentence: The molecular gas reservoir can be replenished through either accretion of cold gas from the halo or wet mergers.
qwen_reason: Base focuses on gas removal suppressing SF; intro discusses replenishment mechanisms. Related topic but different facet, not a direct denial of the suppression mechanism itself.

base_sentence: Gas removal and depletion, including disrupted accretion and stripping of circumgalactic reservoirs, suppress star formation by reducing cold gas supply.
intro_sentence: Such “morphological quenching” can persist over several Gyr, and it is effective even if gas is continually accreted from external sources.
qwen_reason: Base attributes quenching to gas removal; intro describes morphological quenching effective despite accretion. Different mechanism/facet of quenching.

base_sentence: Galaxy environment correlates with morphological transformation and color evolution, with dense regions hosting predominantly quenched early-type systems.
intro_sentence: The primary results are presented in sections § 4.3 through § 4.7 , in which we correlate various morphology parameters with the environment, host halo properties, and internal galaxy characteristics, such as stellar mass, colour, size, and black hole mass.
qwen_reason: Intro states the paper correlates morphology with environment/host properties, which aligns with the base claim that environment correlates with morphological transformation.

base_sentence: Ram-pressure stripping removes gas from galaxies via hydrodynamical interactions with the intracluster medium in dense environments.
intro_sentence: The vibrational properties of bulk crystalline Si have been studied both experimentally using neutron scattering techniques Dolling:63a ; Nilsson:72a as well as theoretically using empirical and first-principles methods.
qwen_reason: Intro discusses vibrational properties of crystalline Silicon; base discusses ram-pressure stripping in galaxies. Completely different domains.
